channel.title: XKey Updates
channel.description: XKey - Vietnamese Input Method for macOS

item.title: Version 1.2.24 (Build 20260611.1)
item.description:
What's New

fix(injection): re-detect method on first keystroke after focus-moving Cmd-chord in browsers

Cmd+L/Cmd+T move focus into the browser address bar (Cmd+F/Cmd+K into

the find/search field), but the injection method is re-detected

asynchronously (AXObserver with a 100ms throttle, mouse-click retries),

so characters typed immediately after the chord could still use the

previous context's method and race the omnibox autocomplete (e.g.

'caau' → 'caâu').

Reuse the one-shot probe pattern from the overlay fix at the injection-

method level: EventTapManager arms a reprobe on focus-moving Cmd-chords

(L/T/N/F/K only — broader chords like Cmd+C/V/W/R don't move focus into

chrome UI and would only add AX cost and sample transitional AX states),

and the next plain keyDown consumes it in shouldProcessEvent,

synchronously re-detecting and confirming the method before the first

character is processed. A keystroke landing within 30ms of the chord

keeps the probe armed instead of consuming it — the browser hasn't

moved focus yet, and re-detecting that early would pin the OLD context's

method. There is deliberately no 'a newer confirmation already landed'

shortcut: async confirmations can carry a pre-chord focus snapshot with

a post-chord timestamp, so timestamps cannot prove freshness.

The reprobe is restricted to browser bundle IDs; other apps keep the

existing event-driven detection. Cost: at most one detectInjectionMethod()

per focus-moving chord; plain typing pays a single comparison.

Xem chi tiết trên GitHub
